M&A New York City, New York Charlotte, North Carolina Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania Job Description At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place. The ideal candidate will have three to four years of experience in transactional tax matters, and experience with private equity transactions is preferred. LL.M. in Taxation is strongly preferred. Patent Prosecution Litigation Rochester, New York New York City, New York Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Troutman Pepper Locke is seeking an Associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our Health Care + Life Sciences Intellectual Property Practice Group resident in our Rochester, Berwyn, Philadelphia, or New York ohice locations. Successful candidates will have experience with patent preparation, prosecution, and litigation. They will be forward thinking, thorough and detail oriented with substantive academic and professional knowledge. They will work well in a team environment and have excellent time management skills which they can utilize both independently and collaboratively in fast-paced situations. They must be licensed in the jurisdiction where they practice and have a background in biology, immunology, molecular biology or biotechnology. CHI Litigation Associate (4th - 7th Year)View Job ↗
2026-03-19
Chicago, Illinois · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 14 elite matches
Show job description
White Collar Chicago, Illinois Paul Hastings is seeking an Associate with four to seven years of experience to join our litigation team in Chicago. This is an opportunity to be part of a rapidly growing practice and work on high-proﬁle matters for a collaborative team that prioritizes associate development and mentorship. Ideal candidates will have white collar experience. Outstanding academic credentials and law ﬁrm or governmental experience are required, and a federal clerkship and law journal experience are strongly preferred. Candidates must also be admitted to the IL Bar. Please submit a cover letter, resume, writing sample, and law school transcript. The salary wage range that we expect to pay for this position is a minimum of $310,000 and a maximum of $420,000 (annually). The actual pay wage may vary based on experience or other relevant factors. Wilmington, Delaware · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 4 elite matches
Show job description
Commercial Wilmington, Delaware Cozen O’Connor is seeking a litigation associate to join its Wilmington, Delaware ohice. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of litigation experience. The candidate should have demonstrated experience in all aspects of litigation, including drafting and reviewing pleadings, legal research and analysis, motion practice, and oral advocacy. This position may support attorneys in various practice areas across the ﬁrm. Must have superior writing skills, strong analytical and research abilities, and excellent communication skills. Admission to the Delaware Bar is required. The salary range for this position is $215,000-$255,000 and represents the Firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting. Actual compensation will be dependent upon a number of factors, including but not limited to, the candidate’s relevant experience and qualiﬁcations. National Security Senior Associate Dechert LLP

Cleveland, Ohio · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 24 elite matches
Show job description
Construction Cleveland, Ohio Ice Miller is seeking an associate with at least three (3) years of experience three (3) years of experience three (3) years of experience in construction law and/or litigation to join our practice. The successful candidate will become part of a collaborative team representing clients in a broad range of construction matters — including contract drafting and negotiation, proactive claims management during project construction, claims avoidance and mediation, arbitration or litigation, and related project development issues. Experience with mechanic’s lien claims is a plus. The ideal candidate will have prior experience in construction law, a solid understanding of construction project operations and administration, strong legal research skills, excellent drafting experience (pleadings, contracts, and other legal documents), and a demonstrated ability to communicate ehectively both orally and in writing. Chicago, Illinois Job Description: The Chicago ohice of Sidley Austin LLP is seeking associates with at least 3 years of experience to join the Litigation Group, which ohers associates the opportunity to work in a variety of practice areas. Candidates must have exceptional academic credentials, strong research and writing skills, and be admitted or eligible to for admission to the Illinois bar. Salaries vary by location and are based on numerous factors, including, but not limited to, the relevant market, skills, experience, and education of the selected candidate. The target salary range for this role is below. Our compensation package also includes bonus eligibility and a comprehensive beneﬁts program. Beneﬁts information can be found here. Commercial Miami, Florida Foley & Lardner is seeking an associate with 4-5 years of business litigation experience to join the Commercial Litigation Practice Group in our Miami ohice. 216 of 228 Construction Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Ice Miller is seeking an associate with at least three (3) years of experience in construction law and/or litigation to join our practice. Patent Prosecution Litigation Rochester, New York New York City, New York Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Troutman Pepper Locke is seeking an Associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our Health Care + Life Sciences Intellectual Property Practice Group resident in our Rochester, Berwyn, Philadelphia, or New York ohice locations. Successful candidates will have experience with patent preparation, prosecution, and litigation. They will be forward thinking, thorough and detail oriented with substantive academic and professional knowledge. They will work well in a team environment and have excellent time management skills which they can utilize both independently and collaboratively in fast-paced situations. They must be licensed in the jurisdiction where they practice and have a background in biology, immunology, molecular biology or biotechnology. Commercial Wilmington, Delaware Cozen O’Connor is seeking a litigation associate to join its Wilmington, Delaware ohice. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of litigation experience. The candidate should have demonstrated experience in all aspects of litigation, including drafting and reviewing pleadings, legal research and analysis, motion practice, and oral advocacy. This position may support attorneys in various practice areas across the ﬁrm. Must have superior writing skills, strong analytical and research abilities, and excellent communication skills. Admission to the Delaware Bar is required. The salary range for this position is $215,000-$255,000 and represents the Firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting. Law-clerk experience in a federal-court clerkship is a plus, but not required. Qualiﬁed candidate must be a persuasive legal writer and oral advocate and will have substantial experience with complex business litigation matters in state and federal courts, including conducting and managing written and document discovery, researching and drafting pleadings, motions and other court ﬁlings, conducting/defending depositions, preparing witnesses for testimony, appearing for status conferences, and arguing motions in both state and federal court. Asset Management AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-02
District of Columbia; New York City, New York · Corporate · 2–7 yrs · 7 elite matches
Show job description
We are looking for associates with private equity and hedge fund experience between Classes of 2024 and 2019 to join our Asset Management team in DC or NY. The Firm pays market salaries to our attorneys. As such, below is the current associate base salary scale: * 1st year: $225,000 * 2nd year: $235,000 * 3rd year: $260,000 * 4th year: $310,000 * 5th year: $365,000 * 6th year: $390,000 * 7th year: $420,000 * 8th year: $435,000 The actual salary offered will be based on a number of factors including but not limited to the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and if applicable, the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job. Salary Range $310,000—$435,000 USD

Greenberg Traurig 2 roles · 6 matches · best 195
Capital Markets Associate (Mid to Senior Level)View Job ↗
2026-02-09
New York City, New York · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a Mid-Level Associate in the Capital Markets Group in New York. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Capital Markets Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements: Ideal candidates will have 5 - 7 years of experience, from a peer BigLaw firm, in public and private equity and debt offerings, including initial public offerings and follow-on offerings, proxy statements and S-4 registration statements in connection with business combination transactions, interfacing with the SEC and other regulators, and familiarity with the ongoing reporting requirements under the ’34 Act for public companies, including 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks and proxy statements. Experience in representing both issuers and underwriters would be beneficial. Candidates also should have day-to-day experience counseling public companies, including with respect to corporate governance matters. Candidates should possess a strong academic background, client management skills and drafting and negotiation skills. Candidates must have the skills to retain and grow existing clients and relationships. Candidates also should be admitted to the New York bar. The expected pay range for this position is: $365,000-$420,000. Salary will be determin

Denver, Colorado · Environmental · 4–6 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for an Associate in the Environmental Practice of our Denver office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements Greenberg Traurig’s Denver office is seeking an Environmental Associate with 4-6 years of environmental, natural resources law and related litigation experience. Qualified applicants must have excellent academic credentials as well as strong research, writing, and analytical skills. Applicants must also have education and practical experience in the areas of air quality, natural resource development, and/or federal or state administrative law. Experience with water quality, water law, energy project permitting, and/or solid and hazardous waste law is strongly preferred. Must be highly self-motivated, able to meet time-sensitive responsibilities and manage multiple work streams, hard-working, responsive and an excellent communicator, flexible, and willing to occasionally travel. Prior experience as a judicial clerk, a national law firm, or a federal or state environmental agency, Department of Justice, or state attorney general’s office is preferred. Applicants must possess strong work ethic and be very team oriented. Applicants must be admitted o

Troutman Pepper 1 role · 1 match · best 169
Transactional Real Estate Associate - AtlantaView Job ↗
2025-10-29
Atlanta, Georgia · Real Estate · 2–5 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Troutman Pepper Locke’s Real Estate practice group is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Associate to join our dynamic team in the Atlanta office. The ideal candidate will have 2 to 5 years of substantive experience in real estate transactional matters, with expertise in real estate debt or equity matters. The successful candidate will handle a variety of real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing, and development projects, and provide legal advice and support on real estate financing, including debt and equity structuring. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating complex real estate documents such as purchase agreements, leases, and financing documents, collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and providing strategic legal guidance, conducting due diligence, and managing closing processes for real estate transactions. The salary range for this position is $235,000 - $310,000, dependent on relevant years of experience. Candidates must be licensed to practice law and in good standing in Georgia. For more information about the practice visit our website here. For inquiries or additional information on this position, please contact Tonja Washington . By applying for this position, you agree and understand that Troutman Pepper Locke will process your Personal Information pursuant to the terms of our Worker and Applicant Global Privacy Notice If you have questions about our data handling practices,

Winston & Strawn 3 roles · 15 matches · best 193
New York Office - Energy and Infrastructure Transactions AssociateView Job ↗
2026-01-28
New York City, New York · Energy · 3–6 yrs · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn’s New York office is looking for a mid-level associate who has experience in the energy (particularly power generation and transmission) and infrastructure sectors to be part of highly regarded team. The work focuses on acquisitions and dispositions of and equity investments in projects and portfolios, development of projects, and financings. Experience should include performing and supervising due diligence, drafting of principal and ancillary agreements, coordinating with specialists (tax, environmental, real estate, regulatory, etc.) and managing closings. All candidates should have excellent academic credentials from an accredited law school, as well as strong organizational, analytical, writing, oral communication, and people skills. Candidate should be someone who thrives in a collaborative environment working closely with other lawyers (both partners and associates) and clients. All candidates must be admitted to practice in New York. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

Richmond, Virginia · Labor & Employment · 2–7 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Litigation Richmond, Virginia Hunton Andrews Kurth LLP seeks an attorney to join its labor and employment team as a non-partnership track salaried 25 of 219 attorney in its Richmond, VA olice. An attorney in this position will be beneﬁts-eligible and will generally have an expected billable hour goal of 1400-1800 hours. The ideal candidate will have at least three years of employment litigation and counseling experience. A successful candidate will have extensive litigation experience; be capable of running and managing cases, communicating, and negotiating with opposing counsel, arguing motions in court; have strong analytical, research and writing skills; and be comfortable advising clients directly and managing deadlines and case dockets. Experience with traditional labor and labor management relations is a plus. Licensure in the jurisdiction of the home olice is required. Virginia bar membership is preferred but, in any event, required within one year of commencing employment. Job

Litigation Tampa, Florida Position Summary Our Tampa olice has an opportunity for a mid-level Associate to join the ﬁrm’s dynamic and long-established practice and represent premier local and national employers. This is an excellent opportunity for associates who are proactive and passionate about assuming a high-level role providing legal service within a sophisticated practice and who are seeking to assume a signiﬁcant role in all aspects of labor and employment law. This role requires extensive contact with clients, governmental agencies, educational institutions and opposing counsel. Duties include all aspects of corporate labor and employment legal representation, but are not limited to, developing creative ideas and pragmatic solutions for client problems, litigation and trial work, and resolving governmental and workplace investigations. Structured Finance New York City, New York District of Columbia Los Angeles, California Chicago, Illinois Charlotte, North Carolina Paul Hasting’ Structured Credit Group is seeking an Associate with two to six years of experience to join one of its CLO teams in NY. The ideal candidate will have experience dealing with credit products and collateralized loan obligation transactions and has represented investment banks (as both arranger and ﬁnancing provider) and investment advisors (as collateral manager and equity investors) in connection with various CLO and related warehouse ﬁnancing transactions. This CLO group is led by a Chambers Ranked “up and coming” partner with opportunity to work on a lean team and have client facing responsibilities and hands-on mentorship. This position may reside in New York, DC, LA, Chicago, or Charlotte, and candidates must be admitted to practice law and in good standing in the jurisdiction where they are hired. Chicago, Illinois Foley & Mansﬁeld, a professional limited liability partnership, is a national law ﬁrm with more than 110 attorneys in olices from coast to coast. We provide legal expertise, creative solutions and extensive trial experience for large corporations, small businesses and individuals. We have an opportunity for a defense litigation attorney to join our Chicago-based general liability team. The ideal candidate will have strong verbal and written communication skills, in addition to good academic credentials. The candidate must be a self-starter and possess the ability to multi-task. Working with others in a team-oriented environment is essential. This position is hybrid with ﬂexibility on the number of days in olice. Qualiﬁcations • 3+ years defense litigation experience • Hands-on experience taking a case ﬁle from inception through resolution • Experience in general liability preferred • Must be admitted to practice in IL Why should you work for Foley Mansﬁeld?
